topic: Meta-question: Will CP on Dem House control be >9% on Jan 14
summary: P(CP strictly > 9% on Jan 14) ≈ 8%. CP has been locked at 8-9% for 73 days with zero exceedances above 9%. Monte Carlo with Bayesian smoothing gives 5-7%, structural factors strongly favor stability.
content:
Post ID 35760 - Underlying question: Will Democrats control House before Jan 3, 2027?

Current CP: 0.09 (9%) as of Jan 3, 2026. Threshold: strictly > 9.00%.

EMPIRICAL: Since Nov 3, CP has only taken values {0.08, 0.09}. Never above 0.09 in 9 observations over 73 days.

MARKOV TRANSITIONS from 0.09: 75% stay at 0.09, 25% drop to 0.08, 0% go above (4 obs).

ACTIVITY: 22 forecasters, ~0.15 updates/day → expect ~1.6 updates in 11-day window.

MONTE CARLO (100K sims): With 4% per-transition P(up), get P(>9%) ≈ 5-7%.
Sensitivity: P(up per transition) of 1%→2.1%, 4%→5.1%, 8%→8.9%, 10%→10.8%.

STRUCTURAL: Long-term downtrend (25%→9%), strong consensus Dems won't control House, no political catalyst expected.

FINAL ESTIMATE: ~8% probability CP will be strictly above 9% on Jan 14.
